audits.status: All critical and high-risk issues resolved before deployment
audits.notes: Aerodrome is a ve(3,3) fork of Velodrome V2. This audit covers the core architecture Aerodrome inherited. Post-engagement reviews conducted May-June 2023.

audits.status: All high and medium severity findings fixed or wrapped contract solutions applied
audits.notes: Public audit contest conducted May 23-30, 2022. Aerodrome benefits from Velodrome's security iteration.
Referenced in security ecosystem but specific Aerodrome audit report not found in public search results. Zellic is known to conduct audits in crypto space.

exploit_history.description: DNS records for .box and .finance domains hijacked via NameSilo insider threat. Users redirected to phishing site. Smart contracts unaffected.
exploit_history.root_cause: Web2 infrastructure compromise - insider threat at NameSilo domain registrar
exploit_history.resolution: Team directed users to decentralized ENS mirrors

exploit_history.impact: ~$300,000 in user losses across two incidents
exploit_history.description: Two separate DNS attacks in late 2023 targeting official website links. Both Velodrome and Aerodrome suffered similar attacks within 3 days.

code_health.governance_mechanism: ve(3,3) model - veAERO holders vote on gauge emissions
code_health.contract_architecture: Fork of Velodrome V2 with immutable core contracts and programmatic value flows
code_health.notes: Core AMM contracts are immutable. Parameter changes controlled via veAERO voting (VotingEscrow NFT). Aero Fed system allows veAERO voters to control weekly emission rates.
code_health.upgrade_path: Immutable core contracts for Pool.sol, Voter.sol, Gauge.sol, etc. Parameter governance via veAERO voting. No permissioned upgrade mechanism for core logic.

dependencies.name: Base L2 Network
dependencies.description: Aerodrome operates on Base, which uses a centralized sequencer operated by Coinbase. Single point of failure.
dependencies.notes: Base experienced 33-minute block production halt in Aug 2025 due to sequencer handover failure. OP Stack component 'Conductor' manages High Availability cluster, but single sequencer model remains structural risk.

dependencies.name: Base Bridge
dependencies.description: Users must bridge assets to Base network. Aerodrome depends on bridge protocols (Wormhole, Coinbase bridge) for cross-chain asset transfers.
dependencies.notes: Multiple bridge protocols used. cbETH, cbBTC trading pairs indicate Coinbase-wrapped asset integration. Liquidity pool depth tied to bridge traffic.

dependencies.name: DNS Infrastructure
dependencies.description: Frontend infrastructure vulnerability exploited in multiple DNS hijacking incidents (2023, 2025).
dependencies.notes: Mitigation: team recommends ENS domains and decentralized mirrors. Inherent Web2 risk affecting many DeFi protocols.

dependencies.name: Ethereum Mainnet (L1)
dependencies.description: Base is an OP Stack L2 settlement layer using Ethereum mainnet for security and data availability.

multisig_treasury.description: Aerodrome team multisig controls treasury and parameter governance
multisig_treasury.signers_threshold: Not publicly disclosed in search results
multisig_treasury.notes: Upgrades require approval from both BaseMultisig1 and OpFoundationOperationsSafe with no delay on upgrades. Specific m-of-n configuration not found in public sources.

veaero_governance.description: veAERO (Vote Escrow AERO) holders control gauge emissions and protocol emissions distribution
veaero_governance.mechanism: ERC-721 compliant NFTs representing escrowed AERO with linear voting power decay
veaero_governance.aero_fed_system: Core roadmap item - veAERO voters control weekly AERO emission rates (increase, decrease, or maintain)

bug_bounty.notes: Velodrome Finance has an active Immunefi bug bounty program. Specific confirmation of Aerodrome's participation not found in searches, but likely given protocol importance.

rationale: Aerodrome Finance inherits security architecture from Velodrome V2, which underwent comprehensive Spearbit audit (Feb-Mar 2023) with 1 critical and 8 high-severity findings—all resolved before deployment. Code4rena contest (May 2022) identified 6 high and 17 medium findings, also fixed. Core contracts are immutable; governance via ve(3,3) model and veAERO voting. No direct smart-contract exploits documented, but suffered DNS/frontend attacks in 2023 and 2025 (Web2 infrastructure compromise, not protocol vulnerability). Dependencies include Base L2 with Coinbase-operated single sequencer (medium risk, evidenced by Aug 2025 outage), bridge infrastructure, and Web2 DNS layer. Multisig governance for treasury/parameters, specific threshold not disclosed.

data_quality.completeness: Good coverage of audit history, exploit events, and dependency architecture. Limited info on specific multisig thresholds and Zellic audit details.
data_quality.sources_quality: All sources are public: GitHub audit reports, Web3 security firms, news from reputable crypto outlets (CoinDesk, BeInCrypto), and official Aerodrome documentation.
gaps: Specific m-of-n multisig configuration not found
Immunefi bug bounty confirmation pending direct verification
